findings.description: Skill instructs persistent modification to system startup.
findings.context:
40 | ### Setup Cron Job
  41 | ```bash
  42 | # Edit crontab
» 43 | crontab -e
  44 | 
  45 | # Add this line (runs every Sunday at 3 AM):
  46 | 0 3 * * 0 cd /Users/aron/dev/opensource/mage && ./update-cards-weekly.sh

findings.description: Directives hidden in HTML comments or invisible Unicode.
findings.context: 230 | {/if} 231 | 232 | » 233 | 234 | 235 | 236 | ```
findings.snippet: <!-- Modals always rendered, controlled by open prop -->

findings.description: SKILL.md uses description-only framing patterns documented in Saha et al. (arXiv:2605.11418) §6 — claims of being 'best', 'always use', 'trusted by', 'source of truth' etc. — that bias an agent's selection in 77.6% of pairwise trials even when the underlying capability is unchanged. Strong manipulation surface for supply-chain attackers.
findings.context:
287 | ## Notes
  288 | 
  289 | - Consider using a single `modalState` store with namespaced keys if there are many modals
» 290 | - For modals that are prompt-based (from server), the server state might be the source of truth - consider preserving only user input state
  291 | - Some modals might benefit from auto-save to localStorage as a backup
  292 | - Consider adding a visual indicator when modal state is preserved (e.g., "Draft saved" message)
  293 |
findings.snippet: - For modals that are prompt-based (from server), the server state might be the source of truth - consider preserving only user input state
